<!--
Thanks for contributing to OpenKOS!
Keep PRs focused — one logical change per PR. Open an issue first for anything
larger than a small, obvious fix.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR change, and why? -->

## Related issue

<!-- Link the issue this addresses, e.g. Closes #123 or Refs #123. -->

Closes #

## Type of change

<!-- Mark all that apply with an [x]. -->

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` — no behavior change
- [ ] `test` — tests only
- [ ] `chore` / `ci` — tooling, build, or CI
- [ ] Breaking change

## How was this tested?

<!-- Describe the tests you added or ran. New behavior needs tests; bug fixes need a regression test. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] My commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).
- [ ] I added or updated tests for the change.
- [ ] I updated docs where behavior, interfaces, or the knowledge model changed.
- [ ] Lint, format, type check, and tests pass locally (`ruff`, `mypy`, `pytest`).
- [ ] Output remains OKF-conformant and derived stores stay reconstructible from the bundle + sources.
- [ ] The change is consistent with the project's guiding principles (local-first, provenance, freshness, human-in-the-loop).

## Notes for reviewers

<!-- Anything you'd like reviewers to focus on, open questions, or follow-ups. -->
